title: All-In Summit: Claire Cormier Thielke on China + Q&A with Flexport's Ryan Petersen
description:
This talk was recorded LIVE at the All-In Summit in Miami and included slides. To watch on YouTube, check out our All-In Summit playlist: https://bit.ly/aisytplaylist

0:00 Claire Cormier Thielke's AIS talk: "Tomorrowland: China Placemaking and the Future of Innovation"

17:40 Claire takes Q&A with the Besties + Ryan Petersen: Understanding the China/US rivalry

24:08 How the Evergrande debt situation impacts the greater Chinese real estate industry, China's still-nascent middle class, How China is subsidizing its negative birth rate

34:30 Ryan Petersen breaks down Flexport's business, the tumultuous past few years, and how DTC might be in major trouble

46:34 Asset-heavy play as a hedge against the popular trend of asset-light, velocity vs. speed, Flexport.org's humanitarian relief shipping, China's influence

analysis.summary: The All-In Summit features Claire Cormier Thielke discussing China's placemaking and innovation, followed by a Q&A with Ryan Petersen on the China/US rivalry and Flexport's business. They also discuss the Evergrande debt situation and its impact on the Chinese real estate industry. The conversation touches on various topics including China's influence, asset-heavy play, and humanitarian relief shipping.

takeaways.text: China is subsidizing its negative birth rate
Evergrande debt situation impacts Chinese real estate
Flexport's business is focused on asset-heavy play
DTC might be in major trouble
Velocity is more important than speed in business
China's influence is significant in global trade
Humanitarian relief shipping is a key aspect of Flexport's business
Asset-heavy play can be a hedge against popular trends

quotes.text: DTC might be in major trouble
quotes.speaker: Ryan Petersen

quotes.text: China is subsidizing its negative birth rate
quotes.speaker: Claire Cormier Thielke

sentiment.notes: The conversation is cautious due to the discussion of the Evergrande debt situation and its potential impact on the global economy. However, the tone remains optimistic about the potential for innovation and growth in China.

notable_moments.description: Claire Cormier Thielke takes Q&A with the Besties + Ryan Petersen
Ryan Petersen breaks down Flexport's business and the tumultuous past few years
